title: NFL executives reportedly ‘expect’ Patriots to acquire A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: NFL executives expect the Patriots to acquire Eagles wide receiver A.J. Brown after June 1.
Philadelphia's trade of A.J. Brown post-June 1 could reduce his dead cap hit from $43 million to $16.4 million.
New England's interest in Brown remains high, with potential trade complications due to Philadelphia's asking price.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: '28 Years Later: The Bone Temple' is now streaming on Netflix, featuring Ralph Fiennes in a standout role.
'Eeephus,' a Massachusetts-filmed baseball movie with a 100% Rotten Tomatoes score, is available on Mubi.
Season 2 of Jon Hamm's dark comedy 'Your Friends and Neighbors' is streaming on Apple TV.

title: Caleb Durbin’s redemption, Willson Contreras’s swagger & other takeaways from Red Sox’ home-opening win
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox defeated Padres 5-2 in the home opener, ending a five-game losing streak.
Caleb Durbin ended a 0-for-34 slump with an RBI single, pleasing manager Alex Cora.
Willson Contreras hit a home run, while Sonny Gray pitched six strong innings.

title: Here are the dates for the Patriots’ offseason workout schedule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots' offseason program begins April 20 with two weeks of conditioning drills, meetings, and rehab work.
OTAs for the Patriots are scheduled for May 27, May 29, June 1-2, June 4, June 8-9, and June 11.
A mandatory three-day minicamp takes place June 15-17, concluding the team's voluntary offseason program.

title: Patriots double up on edge rushers in latest ESPN mock draft
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots' latest ESPN mock draft suggests picking edge rushers Cashius Howell and Gabe Jacas in the first two rounds.
Howell, from Texas A&M, would bring his 11.5 sacks and explosive pass-rushing abilities to New England.
Illinois' Gabe Jacas is considered an NFL-ready run defender with traits comparable to Patriots' Matthew Judon.

title: For a struggling Red Sox team, Friday’s opener at Fenway Park provided a fresh start
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: The Red Sox won their home opener 5-2 against the San Diego Padres at Fenway Park.
Sonny Gray pitched six strong innings, establishing himself as a reliable option for the team.
Willson Contreras hit a 423-foot home run, while Caleb Durbin got his first RBI for the Red Sox.

title: North Andover police working to get body cameras after Kelsey Fitzsimmons shooting
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: North Andover seeks grant funding for body-worn cameras after officer-involved shooting incident without camera evidence.
Former officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons was acquitted in a shooting case due to reasonable doubt linked to lack of camera.
Since 2021, the state awarded nearly $13 million to over 144 local police departments for body camera programs.

title: Xander Bogaerts talks Roman Anthony, pressure from fans in his return to Fenway Park
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Xander Bogaerts returned to Fenway Park as a Padres player, reflecting on changes in the Red Sox team.
Bogaerts received a standing ovation from Red Sox fans, acknowledging his decade-long contribution with two championships.
Bogaerts discussed high expectations on Red Sox prospect Roman Anthony, who signed an eight-year, $130 million contract.

title: Step into Boston’s past this season
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Boston's Freedom Trail attracts over 4 million visitors annually, connecting 16 historical sites along a 2.5-mile path.
Six Flags New England introduces Quantum Accelerator, a dual-launch straddle coaster, opening for all guests on April 17.
Restaurant Week Portsmouth & the Seacoast offers prix fixe menus from April 9-18, with over 30 restaurants participating.

title: Singer Vanessa Carlton’s noise complaint against R.I. neighbor ends up before state Supreme Court
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Vanessa Carlton and husband John McCauley face Rhode Island Supreme Court over noise from neighbors' industrial property.
Court ordered Avagyan and Beausoleil to limit noise to 60 decibels; defendants appealed the injunction.
Neighbors claim noise persists; Supreme Court has yet to rule on noise injunction appeal.
